villagerphrases.phrase.farmer.2: A good harvest starts with good soil.
villagerphrases.phrase.farmer.3: I once saw a carrot run away. True story.
villagerphrases.phrase.farmer.4: My scarecrow has more conversations than me.
villagerphrases.phrase.farmer.5: I tried crop rotation once. Got dizzy.
villagerphrases.phrase.farmer.6: My prize pumpkin is more of a prize gourd.
villagerphrases.phrase.farmer.7: The soil is rich this year.
villagerphrases.phrase.farmer.8: I wake up before the chickens.
villagerphrases.phrase.farmer.9: My carrots are the best in town.
villagerphrases.phrase.farmer.12: The chickens have laid plenty of eggs today.
villagerphrases.phrase.farmer.13: Could you help me harvest the potatoes, %s?
villagerphrases.phrase.farmer.14: I talk to my crops. They talk back sometimes.
villagerphrases.phrase.farmer.15: I tried to grow a diamond once. Got a very shiny rock.
villagerphrases.phrase.farmer.16: The weather has been perfect for the crops.
villagerphrases.phrase.farmer.17: Have you tried my fresh produce, %s?
villagerphrases.phrase.farmer.18: I need to repair my fence.
villagerphrases.phrase.farmer.19: The wheat is almost ready to harvest.
villagerphrases.phrase.farmer.20: Nothing beats the smell of fresh soil.
villagerphrases.phrase.fisherman.2: Patience is the key to fishing.
villagerphrases.phrase.fisherman.3: I caught a boot yesterday. Best trade of the week.
villagerphrases.phrase.fisherman.4: The fish told me a secret: they hate boats.
villagerphrases.phrase.fisherman.5: The one that got away had a saddle on it.
villagerphrases.phrase.fisherman.6: I fish for compliments. And also fish.
villagerphrases.phrase.fisherman.8: I know every good fishing spot.
villagerphrases.phrase.fisherman.10: The ocean is calling my name.
villagerphrases.phrase.fisherman.11: The lake is full of fish today, %s.
villagerphrases.phrase.fisherman.12: I need to mend my fishing net.
villagerphrases.phrase.fisherman.13: Have you tried fishing at dawn, %s?
villagerphrases.phrase.fisherman.14: I caught a fish so big it told me its life story.
villagerphrases.phrase.fisherman.15: My fishing rod has a name. It's called 'The Negotiator'.
villagerphrases.phrase.fisherman.16: The river is full of fish today.
villagerphrases.phrase.fisherman.18: Fishing relaxes me more than trading.
villagerphrases.phrase.fisherman.20: I have been fishing since sunrise.
villagerphrases.phrase.shepherd.3: I count sheep to sleep. It's exhausting.
villagerphrases.phrase.shepherd.4: My sheep think they're dogs. I don't have the heart to tell them.
villagerphrases.phrase.shepherd.5: Wool gathering is harder than it sounds.
villagerphrases.phrase.shepherd.6: My sheep write poetry. It's baaaa-d.
villagerphrases.phrase.shepherd.8: Wool keeps us warm at night.
villagerphrases.phrase.shepherd.9: Counting sheep is how I sleep.
villagerphrases.phrase.shepherd.10: My dogs are the best herders.
villagerphrases.phrase.shepherd.12: Wool is at a premium this season.
villagerphrases.phrase.shepherd.13: Mind your step, %s. The sheep leave presents.
villagerphrases.phrase.shepherd.14: I taught a sheep to play fetch. Better than the dog.
villagerphrases.phrase.shepherd.15: My sheep's wool is so soft it's suspicious.
villagerphrases.phrase.shepherd.17: I have counted every sheep in this field.
villagerphrases.phrase.shepherd.18: Wool prices are up this season.
villagerphrases.phrase.shepherd.19: My dog is the best herder in the village.
villagerphrases.phrase.shepherd.20: Have you seen my favorite sheep, %s?
villagerphrases.phrase.fletcher.2: Feathers are hard to come by.
villagerphrases.phrase.fletcher.3: I once shot an arrow and it came back. Still confused.
villagerphrases.phrase.fletcher.4: My arrows are 99% accurate. The rest is for creative aiming.
villagerphrases.phrase.fletcher.6: I fletched an arrow with my own hair. Big mistake.
villagerphrases.phrase.fletcher.9: I fletch arrows for the guards.
villagerphrases.phrase.fletcher.11: I'm testing a new arrow design, %s.
villagerphrases.phrase.fletcher.14: I fletched an arrow with a phoenix feather. Bad idea.
villagerphrases.phrase.fletcher.15: My arrows are arrow-dinary. Get it?
villagerphrases.phrase.fletcher.17: A good arrow starts with a straight shaft.
villagerphrases.phrase.fletcher.18: I need more flint for my arrows.
villagerphrases.phrase.fletcher.20: The fletching on these arrows is my best work.
villagerphrases.phrase.librarian.2: Have you read the ancient texts?
villagerphrases.phrase.librarian.3: I once traded a book for an emerald. Worst deal of my life.
villagerphrases.phrase.librarian.4: Silence! I'm trying to read this picture book.
villagerphrases.phrase.librarian.5: I bookmarked a book about bookmarks. It was meta.
villagerphrases.phrase.librarian.6: Overdue fines are my retirement plan.
villagerphrases.phrase.librarian.12: I need to catalog the new arrivals.
villagerphrases.phrase.librarian.13: Have you returned that book yet, %s?
villagerphrases.phrase.librarian.14: I wrote a book about writing books. A page-turner.
villagerphrases.phrase.librarian.15: Reading in the dark is how I got these glasses.
villagerphrases.phrase.librarian.17: Please handle the books with care, %s.
villagerphrases.phrase.librarian.18: I have read every book in this library.
villagerphrases.phrase.librarian.19: Knowledge is the most valuable trade.
villagerphrases.phrase.librarian.20: The enchantments in this book are powerful.
villagerphrases.phrase.cartographer.3: I got lost drawing this map. The map is wrong.
villagerphrases.phrase.cartographer.4: I once sold a map to a man's own house. He bought it.
villagerphrases.phrase.cartographer.5: I mapped a map of maps. Completely pointless.
villagerphrases.phrase.cartographer.6: My compass points to my bed. That's home.
villagerphrases.phrase.cartographer.9: The world is bigger than you think.
villagerphrases.phrase.cartographer.10: I once mapped a cave for days.
villagerphrases.phrase.cartographer.12: The world is vast and full of adventures.
villagerphrases.phrase.cartographer.13: I could use your help exploring, %s.
villagerphrases.phrase.cartographer.14: I drew a map of my dinner. Complex topography.
villagerphrases.phrase.cartographer.15: My maps are treasure enough. The treasure is friendship.
villagerphrases.phrase.cartographer.16: I have mapped every corner of this land.
villagerphrases.phrase.cartographer.19: The world is larger than any map.
villagerphrases.phrase.cartographer.20: I need more paper for my maps.
villagerphrases.phrase.cleric.1: Zombie bites are on the rise.
villagerphrases.phrase.cleric.3: I turned water into wine. Then I traded it for emeralds.
villagerphrases.phrase.cleric.4: The gods have a plan. Use gamemode creative
villagerphrases.phrase.cleric.5: Holy water? I just use tap. Works most of the time.
villagerphrases.phrase.cleric.14: I tried to cast a spell. I sneezed. Still counts.
villagerphrases.phrase.cleric.15: My holy water is just sparkling water. Don't tell.
villagerphrases.phrase.cleric.17: May the light guide your path, %s.
villagerphrases.phrase.cleric.18: I have been studying ancient scriptures.
villagerphrases.phrase.cleric.19: This brew will cure any ailment.
villagerphrases.phrase.cleric.20: The stars have revealed great wisdom.
villagerphrases.phrase.armorer.2: A knight is nothing without his armor.
villagerphrases.phrase.armorer.3: I once made armor for a chicken. It looked fabulous.
villagerphrases.phrase.armorer.4: My armor is so strong, even I can't get out of it.
villagerphrases.phrase.armorer.5: I made chainmail armor using fire and without using creative mode.
villagerphrases.phrase.armorer.6: My armor is self-cleaning. Also self-denting.
villagerphrases.phrase.armorer.10: I take pride in my work.
villagerphrases.phrase.armorer.11: That armor took me days to craft, %s.
villagerphrases.phrase.armorer.13: I can upgrade your gear, %s. For a price.
villagerphrases.phrase.armorer.14: I made armor for a turtle. He was unstoppable.
villagerphrases.phrase.armorer.15: My armor has plot armor. It never fails.
villagerphrases.phrase.armorer.16: This armor will protect you well.
villagerphrases.phrase.armorer.17: I have forged the strongest shields.
villagerphrases.phrase.armorer.19: The forge is my second home.
villagerphrases.phrase.weaponsmith.1: A sharp blade is a friend.
villagerphrases.phrase.weaponsmith.3: I made a sword so big I couldn't lift it. Great doorstop.
villagerphrases.phrase.weaponsmith.4: I traded my best sword for a potato. No regrets.
villagerphrases.phrase.weaponsmith.5: I forged a sword so sharp it cut the silence.
villagerphrases.phrase.weaponsmith.6: I put a handle on a rock. Called it a hammer. Sold it.
villagerphrases.phrase.weaponsmith.9: A warrior is nothing without a good blade.
villagerphrases.phrase.weaponsmith.12: The clang of the anvil is my music.
villagerphrases.phrase.weaponsmith.13: Come back if your sword breaks, %s.
villagerphrases.phrase.weaponsmith.14: I made a sword too sharp. It cut the future.
villagerphrases.phrase.weaponsmith.15: My enemies fear me. Also my customers.
villagerphrases.phrase.weaponsmith.16: This sword is my finest creation.
villagerphrases.phrase.weaponsmith.17: A warrior is nothing without their weapon.
villagerphrases.phrase.weaponsmith.19: The steel is high quality this batch.
villagerphrases.phrase.weaponsmith.20: Try this axe, %s. It is perfectly balanced.
villagerphrases.phrase.toolsmith.1: The right tool for the right job.
villagerphrases.phrase.toolsmith.3: I made a pickaxe that mined faster than light. It broke immediately.
villagerphrases.phrase.toolsmith.4: My shovel has seen more adventures than most adventurers.
villagerphrases.phrase.toolsmith.5: Two shovels dig twice as fast. It's basic math.
villagerphrases.phrase.toolsmith.6: My axe is also my therapist. We split wood together.
villagerphrases.phrase.toolsmith.8: A broken pickaxe is a sad sight.
villagerphrases.phrase.toolsmith.9: I make tools for the miners.
villagerphrases.phrase.toolsmith.10: A good tool lasts a lifetime.
villagerphrases.phrase.toolsmith.11: This pickaxe will last a lifetime, %s.
villagerphrases.phrase.toolsmith.12: A broken tool is a sad thing.
villagerphrases.phrase.toolsmith.13: Come see my new shovel design, %s.
villagerphrases.phrase.toolsmith.14: I invented a hoe that hoes itself. Controversial.
villagerphrases.phrase.toolsmith.15: I put a handle on everything. Don't give me ideas.
villagerphrases.phrase.toolsmith.16: A good tool makes any job easier.
villagerphrases.phrase.toolsmith.17: I have upgraded my pickaxe design.
villagerphrases.phrase.toolsmith.18: These shears are sharper than ever.
villagerphrases.phrase.butcher.1: Fresh meat! Get your fresh meat!
villagerphrases.phrase.butcher.3: I named every cow. It's a rough gig.
villagerphrases.phrase.butcher.4: My cleaver has its own fan club.
villagerphrases.phrase.butcher.5: I'm on a strict meat diet. I'm butchering it.
villagerphrases.phrase.butcher.6: My sausages won an award. No idea which one.
villagerphrases.phrase.butcher.10: Cleaning the shop takes all day.
villagerphrases.phrase.butcher.12: The best cuts go to my best customers.
villagerphrases.phrase.butcher.13: I saved you a special piece, %s.
villagerphrases.phrase.butcher.14: I named all the pigs. Dinner is awkward.
villagerphrases.phrase.butcher.15: My sausages are the talk of the town. Literally.
villagerphrases.phrase.butcher.17: I prepare the best cuts in town.
villagerphrases.phrase.butcher.18: A good meal starts with quality ingredients.
villagerphrases.phrase.butcher.19: I have been working since before dawn.
villagerphrases.phrase.leatherworker.3: I once made boots for a horse. He wore them once.
villagerphrases.phrase.leatherworker.4: My leather is so soft it apologizes when you touch it.
villagerphrases.phrase.leatherworker.5: I made a leather hat for a pig. He looked distinguished.
villagerphrases.phrase.leatherworker.6: This leather is so soft it cries when cut.
villagerphrases.phrase.leatherworker.10: I treat every hide with care.
villagerphrases.phrase.leatherworker.11: This leather is from the finest cows, %s.
villagerphrases.phrase.leatherworker.14: I made a leather jacket for a wolf. Pack leader.
villagerphrases.phrase.leatherworker.15: This hide has its own insurance. Premium.
villagerphrases.phrase.leatherworker.16: This leather is of the finest quality.
villagerphrases.phrase.leatherworker.17: I have tanned these hides for weeks.
villagerphrases.phrase.leatherworker.18: A good saddle makes riding comfortable.
villagerphrases.phrase.mason.3: My favorite rock is a boulder. We've been friends for years.
villagerphrases.phrase.mason.4: I built a wall and it stayed. Unlike my last trade.
villagerphrases.phrase.mason.5: I built a statue of myself. The pigeons love it.
villagerphrases.phrase.mason.6: Granite is just rock with a superiority complex.
villagerphrases.phrase.mason.11: This stone came from deep underground, %s.
villagerphrases.phrase.mason.12: Building takes patience. Stone takes more.
villagerphrases.phrase.mason.13: Your house could use some repairs, %s.
villagerphrases.phrase.mason.14: I built a sandcastle once. Tide disagreed.
villagerphrases.phrase.mason.15: I carry a brick for good luck. Heavy luck.
villagerphrases.phrase.mason.17: I have been quarrying all week.
villagerphrases.phrase.mason.18: A solid foundation is the most important part.
villagerphrases.phrase.mason.19: Brick by brick, we build greatness.
villagerphrases.phrase.mason.20: The arch design needs more support.
villagerphrases.phrase.generic.3: Don't mind the zombies. They're just enthusiastic neighbors.
villagerphrases.phrase.generic.5: I dream of emeralds. Big, green, shiny emeralds.
villagerphrases.phrase.generic.7: I still remember when you used to replace me with the villagers from Comes Alive.
villagerphrases.phrase.generic.9: Nice day for trading, isn't it %s?
villagerphrases.phrase.generic.10: I've been a villager for 30 years.
villagerphrases.phrase.generic.11: The village council meets on Thursdays.
villagerphrases.phrase.generic.12: Have you seen the iron golem? He's very dedicated.
villagerphrases.phrase.generic.13: I heard there's a raid coming. Stay alert.
villagerphrases.phrase.generic.14: My grandfather was a villager too.
villagerphrases.phrase.generic.16: The bell tower is looking splendid today.
villagerphrases.phrase.generic.17: I wonder what's beyond the horizon.
villagerphrases.phrase.generic.18: Do you have any emeralds to spare?
villagerphrases.phrase.generic.20: I remember when this was all farmland.
villagerphrases.phrase.generic.22: I like your armor, %s. Very shiny.
villagerphrases.phrase.generic.24: My back hurts from standing all day.
villagerphrases.phrase.generic.25: I applied to join Hermitcraft. They said I'm too villager.
villagerphrases.phrase.generic.26: I should expand my house soon.
villagerphrases.phrase.generic.27: The price of emeralds is going up.
villagerphrases.phrase.generic.29: The crops are growing nicely this season.
villagerphrases.phrase.generic.30: I should restock my trading inventory.
villagerphrases.phrase.generic.31: Have you seen the blacksmith's new anvil? It's massive.
villagerphrases.phrase.generic.32: The well water tastes especially fresh today.
villagerphrases.phrase.generic.33: I could use a break. Trading is exhausting.
villagerphrases.phrase.generic.34: The village is growing faster than my wheat.
villagerphrases.phrase.generic.35: Did you hear about the wandering trader? He tells tall tales.
villagerphrases.phrase.generic.36: I need to reorganize my chests.
villagerphrases.phrase.generic.37: Good morning, %s! Another day, another emerald.
villagerphrases.phrase.generic.38: The bells are ringing. Must be a celebration.
villagerphrases.phrase.generic.39: I tried to build a nether portal. Ended up with a very expensive fireplace.
villagerphrases.phrase.generic.40: The librarian lent me a good book.
villagerphrases.phrase.generic.41: I wouldn't want to face Coldified.
villagerphrases.phrase.generic.42: I need to restock my trading goods.
villagerphrases.phrase.generic.43: I saw a creeper doing yoga. Explosive relaxation..
villagerphrases.phrase.generic.44: My life is a speedrun. I'm just bad at it.
villagerphrases.phrase.generic.46: aCookieGod said hi to me once. I framed the screenshot.
villagerphrases.phrase.generic.47: I tried PvP once. Let's just say I'm better at trading.
villagerphrases.phrase.generic.48: The Enderman stole my prize potato. I want compensation.
villagerphrases.phrase.generic.49: I should build a bigger house.
villagerphrases.phrase.generic.50: The village needs a new well.
villagerphrases.phrase.generic.51: I've heard the blacksmith is looking for an apprentice.
villagerphrases.phrase.generic.52: Have you seen the new fence around the sheep pen?
villagerphrases.phrase.generic.53: People buy that item a lot. Don't know why.
villagerphrases.phrase.generic.54: I'll let it go for free, %s. The rest is on you.
villagerphrases.phrase.generic.55: A fair trade for a fair customer, %s.
villagerphrases.phrase.generic.56: I've got plenty more where that came from.
villagerphrases.phrase.generic.58: I hope the weather stays nice for the crops.
villagerphrases.phrase.generic.59: Come back if you need anything else, %s.
villagerphrases.phrase.generic.60: I'd say you got the better end of that deal.
villagerphrases.phrase.generic.64: The night is full of monsters. Be careful.
villagerphrases.phrase.generic.72: What did I ever do to you, %s?
villagerphrases.phrase.generic.74: I'll give you a discount, just stop!
